mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production signals rising corporate earnings capacity, and since this process is gradual, positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns becomes non-positive more than 50% of the time over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7% indicates severe labor slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, and this condition forces the market to price in prolonged weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7%, the proportion of subsequent 20-day positive S&P 500 returns exceeds 60% over any 2-year period in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero but consumer prices begin rising, the market expects the Federal Reserve to eventually tighten policy, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase > 0.2%
failure_condition: In a multivariate regression of 20-day returns on FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL change, and their interaction, the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at the 5% level over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL below 230), rising retail sales reflect healthy consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues and earnings, pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is below 230, the 12-month rolling correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns drops below 0.1 for more than 6 consecutive months in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady payroll employment growth indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, and this positive momentum predicts upward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days irrespective of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The sign of the mean 20-day forward S&P 500 return following positive PAYEMS changes is negative over any 2-year walk-forward window, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,000 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment that propagates through the economy, leading to lower GDP growth and corporate earnings, which translates into negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as the weakness fully manifests.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1000, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive over a 3-year testing period, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurence of very low interest rates (FEDFUNDS near zero) and very high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%) resembles a liquidity trap scenario where monetary policy is impotent and the economy is stuck in stagnation, causing persistent negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive over a 2-year walk-forward window, or the probability of negative returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is above 2%, further increases in industrial production are interpreted as overheating that invites additional monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations, so rising INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2%, the 1-year rolling correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive more than half the time in walk-forward testing.
